<!-- topic: compile -->
<!-- sources: ADR-007, ADR-008, ADR-009 -->
<!-- compiled_by: gov-compile v0.1.0 -->
<!-- compiled_at: 2026-04-17T23:18:49Z -->

# Compile

- Generated governance.md MUST have `compile_schema_version` in YAML frontmatter, set to the integer constant declared in `commands/gov/compile.md`. NEVER stamp edikt marketing version into this field. (ref: ADR-007)
- `/edikt:doctor` MUST treat missing `compile_schema_version` as schema version 1 (legacy). If the file's schema version is less than the current constant, recommend `/edikt:gov:compile` to regenerate. (ref: ADR-007)
- Bump `COMPILE_SCHEMA_VERSION` in `commands/gov/compile.md` ONLY when the output structure changes in a way that older tooling cannot read. Bug fixes, prose changes, and directive updates do NOT bump the schema. (ref: ADR-007)
- `compiled_by` and `compiled_at` MUST remain in HTML comments (not YAML frontmatter). They are human diagnostics, not programmatic contracts. Never enforce equality on them in tests or in doctor/upgrade logic. (ref: ADR-007)
- The directives sentinel block MUST contain three lists: `directives`, `manual_directives`, `suppressed_directives`. `manual_directives` and `suppressed_directives` are optional (default empty). (ref: ADR-008)
- `<artifact>:compile` commands MUST regenerate only the `directives:` list. They MUST NOT read, write, or touch `manual_directives:` or `suppressed_directives:`. (ref: ADR-008)
- `gov:compile` MUST read all three lists from every artifact and compute `effective = (directives - suppressed_directives) âˆª manual_directives` as the final rule set written to governance.md. (ref: ADR-008)
- `<artifact>:compile` MUST write `source_hash`, `directives_hash`, and `compiler_version` into the directives block on every write. (ref: ADR-008)
- `source_hash` MUST be SHA-256 of the artifact body with the directives block excluded, normalized (CRLFâ†’LF, trailing whitespace stripped). (ref: ADR-008)
- `directives_hash` MUST be SHA-256 of the auto `directives:` list items joined by `\\n`. `manual_directives:` and `suppressed_directives:` MUST NOT be hashed. (ref: ADR-008)
- `<artifact>:compile` MUST use the fast-path skip when both hashes match. No Claude call, no writes. (ref: ADR-008)
- `<artifact>:compile` MUST trigger the hand-edit interview when `source_hash` matches but `directives_hash` does not. (ref: ADR-008)
- The hand-edit interview MUST ask one question per detected edit and wait for user response. No silent auto-resolution. (ref: ADR-008)
- Headless mode (`EDIKT_HEADLESS=1` or no `/dev/tty`) MUST fail loud with `--strategy=regenerate` and `--strategy=preserve` override flags documented. NEVER silently resolve. (ref: ADR-008)
- `<artifact>:new` MUST auto-chain to `<artifact>:compile` at the end of its workflow so new artifacts have populated directives immediately. (ref: ADR-008)
- v0.2.x artifacts without the new fields MUST continue to work unchanged. First compile after upgrade writes the new fields; no manual migration required. (ref: ADR-008)
- The schema applies symmetrically to ADRs, invariants, and guidelines. All three MUST have identical block structure and processing rules. (ref: ADR-008)
- The formal name for an invariant governance artifact is 'Invariant Record'. The short form is 'INV'. File naming is INV-NNN-short-title.md. Never use 'IR' as a short form. (ref: ADR-009)
- Invariant Records MUST contain the six-section body structure: Statement, Rationale, Consequences of violation, Implementation (optional), Anti-patterns (optional), Enforcement. An Invariant Record without an Enforcement section is invalid. (ref: ADR-009)
- Invariant Records describe CONSTRAINTS, not IMPLEMENTATIONS. Use the test 'if our tech stack changed tomorrow, would this still apply?' to verify the level of abstraction. Implementation-specific rules belong in ADRs. (ref: ADR-009)
- Invariant Records are NOT derived from ADRs. They are independent artifacts. When an invariant references an ADR, the reference goes in the Rationale or Implementation section as prose, not as a structured frontmatter field. (ref: ADR-009)
- When documenting Invariant Records (in docs, website, or code comments), transparently label the term as 'an edikt convention, not an external standard'. Do not claim external authority. (ref: ADR-009)
- Guidelines do NOT receive a coined term in v0.3.0. They remain as 'guideline' without a formal short form. (ref: ADR-009)
